meta.title: Beyond Evaluation: Techniques to Mitigate CSP and DTP
meta.author: Shaurya Sharthak

shapes.text:
Chhattisgarh Swami Vivekanand Technical University
University Teaching Department
Department of Computer Science and Engineering

Beyond Evaluation: Techniques to Mitigate Code-Switching
and Domain Transfer Penalties in Hindi/Hinglish CoT Reasoning

Shaurya Sharthak (300012722059)  |  Daneshwar Prasad (300012722008)  |  Vishal Verma (300012722049)
CSE-AI

Guided By
Mrs. Basanti Minj
Associate Professor, Department of CSE

items: Introduction & Motivation
Research Objectives
Problem Statement
Baseline Findings (7th Sem)
Literature Survey
Research Methodology
FHA-CoT Dataset
Six Mitigation Techniques
Results & Discussion
Conclusion
References
LLM reasoning research is overwhelmingly English-centric
Hinglish is the de facto language of digital India (1.4B+ people)
Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning enables transparent, step-by-step problem solving
Critical gap: no robust CoT systems for Hindi/Hinglish

slides.title: Research Objectives
items: Develop 6 concrete mitigation techniques for Code-Switching Penalty (CSP) and Domain Transfer Penalty (DTP)
Expand FHA-CoT corpus from 25K to 50K samples via controlled synthetic augmentation
Techniques: Embedding LoRA, Curriculum Learning, Data Augmentation, Cross-Lingual Alignment, Domain Adapters, DPO Training
Build a combined pipeline demonstrating measurable penalty reduction with preserved CoT faithfulness
Provide a reproducible framework for robust multilingual reasoning in the Indian language sphere
Code-Switching Penalty: 10.2% avg FAA drop (Hindi to Hinglish)
Domain Transfer Penalty: 10.35% FAA drop (General to Spiritual)
Compounding effect: worst case 20.3% total drop
12.4% Correct-Unfaithful rate in hardest split

cards.title: CoT Reasoning
cards.body: Wei et al. (2022): Step-by-step reasoning via prompting. Few-Shot, Zero-Shot, and Instruction-Tuned variants.

cards.title: LLaMA Models
cards.body: Touvron et al. (2023): Open-weight foundation models. Llama-3-8B as our base for fine-tuning.

cards.title: PEFT / QLoRA
cards.body: Dettmers et al. (2023): Quantized LoRA enabling fine-tuning on consumer GPUs (RTX 4090, 24GB).

cards.title: Indic NLP Gaps
cards.body: Ahuja et al. (2023): Systematic underperformance on Indian languages. Code-mixing remains unsolved.

slides.title: FHA-CoT Dataset
items: Foundational Hindi/Hinglish Chain-of-Thought Corpus
25K samples expanded to 50K via controlled augmentation
3 cognitive domains: General, Medical, Spiritual (Gita)
2 linguistic variants: Hindi (Devanagari) + Hinglish (Roman)
Instruction-Rationale-Answer triplets with faithfulness scoring

cards.title: 1. Embedding LoRA
cards.body: Dual-adapter targeting tokenization bottleneck for code-mixed input

cards.title: 2. Curriculum Learning
cards.body: Progressive domain adaptation: easy to hard

slides.title: Technique 1: Embedding-Level LoRA Adapters
items: Dual-adapter design: Embedding LoRA + Attention LoRA
Targets the tokenization bottleneck: Hindi = 3.2 tok/word vs English = 1.3
Rank 64: best trade-off (12.6M trainable params, 0.16% of model)
CSP reduction: avg -2.0% across all domains
Largest gain in Spiritual Hinglish (+3.5% FAA)

slides.title: Technique 2: Curriculum Learning
items: 3-stage progressive training: General then Medical then Spiritual
Reduces catastrophic forgetting by establishing stable representations
Spiritual DTP: 10.35% to 7.6% (-26.1%)
Difficulty hierarchy preserved but gaps narrowed
Best for Domain Transfer Penalty reduction

slides.title: Technique 3: Synthetic Data Augmentation
items: Controlled code-mixing algorithm for Hinglish generation
CMI target range: 0.3-0.5 (natural code-switching)
Hinglish training data doubled: 12.5K to 25K samples
Quality filtering via perplexity thresholds
Consistent +0.9-3.9% FAA improvement across all splits

slides.title: Technique 4: Cross-Lingual Alignment Loss
items: Contrastive loss mapping Hindi and Hinglish representations closer
Most effective individual technique for CSP reduction (-2.1%)
Minimal impact on Hindi-only splits (+0.6%)
Reduces representation gap in embedding space
Mathematical framework for cross-lingual transfer

slides.title: Technique 5: Domain-Specific Adapters
items: Per-domain LoRA adapters with learned routing mechanism
Most effective for DTP: Spiritual 10.35% to 6.3% (-39.1%)
Specialized reasoning pathways for each cognitive domain
Router network selects optimal adapter at inference
5% latency overhead at inference time

slides.title: Technique 6: Faithfulness-Aware DPO Training
items: Direct Preference Optimization for reasoning quality
Trains model to prefer logically sound over merely correct
Correct-Unfaithful rate: 12.4% to 7.8% (-37.1%)
Modest FAA gain (+0.3%) but major qualitative improvement
Only technique directly targeting the 'illusion of competence'

slides.title: Full Mitigation Pipeline
slides.caption: Combined 6-technique mitigation pipeline: Embedding LoRA, Curriculum Learning, Data Augmentation, Cross-Lingual Alignment, Domain Adapters, and DPO Training applied sequentially

slides.title: Results: Faithfulness Improvement
items: CoT Faithfulness: 3.5 to 4.3 (5-point scale)
Correct-Unfaithful rate: 12.4% to 4.2%
DPO most effective for faithfulness
Domain Adapters most effective for DTP
Cross-Lingual Alignment most effective for CSP

slides.title: Conclusion
items: CSP and DTP penalties identified in 7th semester are not insurmountable
Combined pipeline: 52.9% CSP reduction, 50.7% DTP reduction
Best individual: Alignment (CSP), Domain Adapters (DTP), DPO (Faithfulness)
FHA-CoT dataset expanded to 50K with controlled augmentation
Reproducible framework for robust multilingual reasoning
Future: Other Indic languages, multi-modal reasoning, real-time adaptive models

slides.title: References
items: [1] J. Wei et al., Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in LLMs, NeurIPS, 2022
[2] H. Touvron et al., LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation LLMs, arXiv, 2023
[3] T. Dettmers et al., QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs, NeurIPS, 2023
[4] K. Cobbe et al., Training Verifiers to Solve Math Word Problems, arXiv, 2021
[5] K. Ahuja et al., IndicNLP Suite for Indian Languages, ACL, 2023
[6] R. Rafailov et al., Direct Preference Optimization, NeurIPS, 2023
[7] FHA-CoT Dataset: huggingface.co/fhai50032/datasets
